query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Elon Musk Net Worth & Tesla/SpaceX Trajectory — Early July 2026

**Has it recovered above $1 trillion? Yes — but narrowly and fragilely.** As of July 2–3, 2026, real-time trackers place Musk at **~$1.05–1.10 trillion**, back above the trillion-dollar line and still the world's richest person (Larry Page distant #2 at ~$296B). The last precise Bloomberg Billionaires Index figure available is **$1.01T "as of June 2026."**

**The late-June selloff:** Musk was a trillionaire for less than two weeks. From a mid-June peak of **~$1.45T**, his net worth fell $300–350 billion and dropped **below $1T several times around June 22–25**, driven by SpaceX collapsing from post-IPO highs plus Tesla weakness in a broad tech selloff.

**SpaceX (SPCX):** IPO'd June 12 (largest ever, priced $135, closed ~$161). Peaked $225.64 (June 16) → troughed $153 (June 25, –32%) → **$162.00 on July 2 (+2.83%), ~$2.13T market cap.** Musk owns ~42%, so SPCX dominates his net-worth swings.

**Tesla (TSLA):** **$393.45 on July 2, down 7.49% that day**, ~$1.48T market cap. Recovered from a $375 low (June 25) to $425 (July 1) before the sharp drop. 6-month return –12.89%, ~21% below its 52-week high. Musk owns ~20%.

**Key caveat:** He's oscillating right at the trillion-dollar threshold rather than sitting firmly above it — SPCX's extreme volatility and Tesla's –7.5% July 2 drop mean daily moves can push him just under $1T. Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been recorded.
